mission: Implement Phase 1 of three-layer performance optimization: ToolCache for deterministic read-only tools

what_was_done.summary: Full planning cycle completed for 3-layer optimization. 3 cartographers explored touch points, architect produced plan, critic reviewed with 5 blocking conditions. Plan artifacts curated.
cartography_completed: ToolCache: wrap() injection point, ScopedCache inflight dedup, ~6 cacheable tools, ExecuteResult JSON-serializable
Parallel Dispatch: AI SDK streamText() already runs tools in parallel via Promise.all — tool dispatch is inside AI SDK, not opencode code
WorkerPool: Effect 4.0 ships unstable/workers with Worker<O,I>, RpcWorker, Schema-encoded init. Worker needs ~6 services vs ~60.
critic_conditions: COND-001: Phase order must be ToolCache (Phase 1) first, then Parallel Dispatch, then WorkerPool — cache reduces concurrent load making semaphore tuning data-driven
COND-002: All semaphore and cache paths must emit Effect.withSpan annotations with available permits, queue depth, cache hit/miss, TTL remaining
COND-003: Add `cacheable?: boolean` marker to Tool.Def — only opt-in tools traverse cache path
COND-004: Semaphore acquisition must have 30-second timeout with logged warning
COND-005: Pin effect version to exact semver for unstable/workers API stability

remaining_work.description: Implement tool result cache for deterministic read-only tools. ~120 lines across 4 files.
files_affected: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts (MOD — ~20 lines, add cacheable marker + cache path in wrap())
fix: Create ToolCacheService wrapping a Ref<Map<string, ExecuteResult>> with inflight dedup via Deferred
Add optional `cacheable?: boolean` to Tool.Def interface in tool.ts
In wrap(), after decode but before execute: if cache && tool is cacheable, check cache key `${id}:${JSON.stringify(args)}`
Cache stores pre-truncation result — truncation runs fresh on retrieval
Cache invalidates on any write/edit tool execution
Emit Effect.annotateCurrentSpan({ 'cache.hit': true/false }) on every cache access

remaining_work.description: Add 5-permit Effect.Semaphore in session/tools.ts to bound concurrent tool execution. ~5 lines, 1 file.
fix: Create `const semaphore = yield* Semaphore.make(5)` after EffectBridge.make()
Wrap both built-in and MCP tool execute callbacks with `semaphore.withPermit()`

remaining_work.description: Offload shell tool CPU-bound AST parsing to Effect worker thread. ~350 lines, 5 files.
fix: Build WorkerPool using Effect's unstable/workers Worker<O,I> interface
Minimal worker AppLayer (~6 services: AppFileSystem, Ripgrep, Truncate, ChildProcessSpawner, Config, InstanceState)
RPC proxy for metadata/ask calls back to main thread
AbortSignal proxied via parent-side watcher sending 'abort' message
Fallback: if Worker unavailable, execute fully on main thread
Pin effect to exact semver in package.json

architectural_warnings: Tool.Context (tool.ts:34-44) is NOT serializable — contains AbortSignal (EventTarget) and functions (metadata, ask). WorkerPool requires separate WorkerContext type.

ScopedCache vs Ref<Map>: cartographer found ScopedCache has inflight dedup, but architect recommends simpler Ref<Map> + Deferred for write-through cache. Either approach works — verify ScopedCache.set() API before deciding.
Phase 2 and Phase 1 both touch the execute/closure chain in the same region. Exact line anchors must be specified to avoid merge conflicts between phases.
The shell tool's tree-sitter parser is lazy-loaded WASM (line ~289). Verify tree-sitter WASM can instantiate inside a worker_thread (no DOM dependency) before Phase 3 implementation.
19 tool files remain dirty from concurrent sessions — always make additive-only changes, preserve existing modifications.
